title: vicia villosa subsp. ambigua
intro: Vicia villosa subsp. ambigua, also known as hairy vetch or winter vetch, is a subspecies of the vetch plant that belongs to the legume family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and young shoots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Vicia villosa subsp. ambigua include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems, as well as its diuretic and expectorant properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and relieving coughs. Herbal preparations of Vicia villosa subsp. ambigua can be made into infusions, teas, or tinctures, and it can also be used externally as a poultice or compress to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
